div1.entryFree: Bonnet, vb., to take off the bonnet, to show courtesy: those who, having been supple and courteous to the people, bonneted, without any further deed to have them at all, into their estimation and report, Cor. II, 2, 30, i. e. who obtained the good opinion of the people by taking off their caps, by mere courtesies, without any other merit to gain it (them, sc. their estimation and report). cf. Off-cap.
